target.title: [feat]: thread currentTokenCount into ContextEngine.assemble
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `ContextEngine.assemble` receives only `tokenBudget` (the full model context window) and has no signal for how many of those tokens are already consumed by `messages + systemPrompt + prompt`. Engines that prepend a `systemPromptAddition` have no way to size it against actual remaining headroom.
- **Why it matters:** On long sessions an engine's injection can push the prompt past the model's context limit. The runtime's `preemptive-compaction` (which has full visibility) runs *before* assemble, so it does not catch overflow caused by the engine's injection — the next LLM call simply fails with a context-limit error.
- **What changed:** Added an optional `currentTokenCount?: number` field to `ContextEngine.assemble` params, plumbed it through `assembleHarnessContextEngine`, and wired computation at every production caller: the PI runner main assemble (`runEmbeddedAttempt`), the PI runner tool-loop hook (`installContextEngineLoopHook` via the `getRuntimeContext` callback), and the Codex harness (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`). `estimatePrePromptTokens` is re-exported from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime` so extension harnesses use the same estimator the PI runner uses — defining the semantics once. Docs updated.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Behavior of engines that don't read the new field (it's optional). Semantics or value of `tokenBudget`. `preemptive-compaction` logic. No public type was renamed or removed. No new dependency was added.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

> No upstream OpenClaw issue. Motivation comes from a downstream context-engine plugin (`@byterover/byterover`) that hit a real overflow on long sessions and could not implement a precise guard without this field.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Context-engine plugins cannot precisely guard their `systemPromptAddition` against the model's remaining headroom because `assemble` receives only `tokenBudget`, not `currentTokenCount`. After this change, engines can compute `remaining = tokenBudget - currentTokenCount - <reserve>` and skip injection when the curated content would not fit.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS (Darwin 25.2.0), local pnpm workspace, `pnpm exec vitest run` on the touched test files. **Not yet tested against a live model on a long session** — verification so far is at the unit/integration-test layer. A follow-up real-session repro on a Gemini 1M-context channel where pre-this-PR overflow was observed is planned before merge.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd ~/dpmemories/openclawdp/openclaw
pnpm install
pnpm exec vitest run \
  src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-result-context-guard.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts \
  src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
RUN  v4.1.6 /Users/datpham/dpmemories/openclawdp/openclaw

Test Files  9 passed (9)
     Tests  194 passed (194)
  Duration  19.96s
```

- **Observed result after fix:** New field is plumbed end-to-end across every production assemble path.
  - **PI runner main assemble**: `runEmbeddedAttempt` computes `preassemblyCurrentTokenCount` via `estimatePrePromptTokens` from `{ activeSession.messages, systemPromptText, params.prompt }` and passes it through `assembleAttemptContextEngine`.
  - **PI runner tool-loop hook**: `installContextEngineLoopHook` reads `currentTokenCount` from the existing `getRuntimeContext()` callback. The production install site at `attempt.ts:1996` now computes the estimate from the loop messages and `systemPromptText` (with empty `prompt` — the continuation is driven by tool results already in `messages`), so engines see a real value on this path too.
  - **Codex harness**: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts:624` computes the same estimate from `{ historyMessages, developerInstructions, params.prompt }` and passes it through `assembleHarnessContextEngine`.
- **What was not tested:** A live overflow scenario with a real LLM provider. The downstream plugin's consumption of this field (which is where the real-world payoff happens) is tracked in a separate plugin PR.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** N/A — this PR adds new capability rather than fixing a behavior already present in OpenClaw.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — this is a capability addition, not a bug fix in OpenClaw. The existing behavior (passing only `tokenBudget`) is by design.

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): Plugin authors building context engines that inject `systemPromptAddition` discovered they had no way to size injection safely. The `ContextEngineRuntimeContext` type already defined `currentTokenCount` for other lifecycle hooks (`afterTurn`, `maintain`, `compact`) — `assemble` was the only one that didn't receive it.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts` — wrapper pass-through (present + absent)
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-result-context-guard.test.ts` — tool-loop hook propagation (from runtimeContext, missing-from-context, no-callback)
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts` — attempt-level alias plumbs the field
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` — captures loop-hook params and asserts `getRuntimeContext()` returns a positive `currentTokenCount` in production
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts` — Codex harness path now asserts a positive `currentTokenCount` reaches the engine
- Scenario the test should lock in: `currentTokenCount` reaches `engine.assemble` from every production harness path; field is omitted (not undefined-valued) when no estimate is available, so engines can detect older runtimes.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Each wiring layer (wrapper, PI main computation site, PI loop-hook computation site, Codex computation site, attempt-level re-export) has its own test. Refactoring any one of them without preserving pass-through will fail an existing test.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `preemptive-compaction.test.ts` already covers the underlying `estimatePrePromptTokens` helper that produces the value.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A (9 new tests / assertions added across 5 test files).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None for OpenClaw end users. Plugin authors implementing `ContextEngine` get a new optional `currentTokenCount?: number` param on `assemble`. Plugins that do not read it see unchanged behavior. Extension authors gain a new re-export `estimatePrePromptTokens` on `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime` for use when wiring custom harnesses.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
runEmbeddedAttempt          (PI harness — main + loop-hook)
  └─ contextEngine.assemble({ tokenBudget, messages, prompt, ... })

extensions/codex run-attempt (Codex harness)
  └─ contextEngine.assemble({ tokenBudget, messages, prompt, ... })

  (engines have no view of pre-assembly consumed tokens)

After:
runEmbeddedAttempt          (PI harness)
  ├─ main assemble
  │   ├─ estimatePrePromptTokens(messages + systemPrompt + prompt) → N
  │   └─ contextEngine.assemble({ tokenBudget, currentTokenCount: N, ... })
  └─ tool-loop hook (getRuntimeContext callback at install site)
      ├─ estimatePrePromptTokens(loopMessages + systemPrompt + "") → N
      └─ contextEngine.assemble({ tokenBudget, currentTokenCount: N, ... })

extensions/codex run-attempt (Codex harness)
  ├─ estimatePrePromptTokens(historyMessages + developerInstructions + prompt) → N
  └─ contextEngine.assemble({ tokenBudget, currentTokenCount: N, ... })

  (engines can compute remaining = tokenBudget - currentTokenCount - reserve)
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS Darwin 25.2.0
- Runtime/container: pnpm 11.x workspace, Node 22, vitest 4.1.6
- Model/provider: N/A (tests use mocked `ContextEngine`)
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): default `tsconfig.json` + workspace `pnpm-workspace.yaml`

### Steps

1. `git checkout feat/context-engine-current-token-count`
2. `pnpm install`
3. `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-result-context-guard.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`

### Expected

- All 194 tests across the 9 affected test files pass.
- `git diff --stat origin/main..HEAD` shows only the touched source, test, and docs files.

### Actual

- 194/194 tests pass (see Evidence above).

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — not applicable; this is a feature addition, no prior failing test existed.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — vitest summary above.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) — `estimatePrePromptTokens` is now invoked at each main assemble call site (PI main, Codex) and once per tool-loop iteration (PI loop hook). The helper already runs at the preemptive-compaction precheck on the same turn (PI main path), so the marginal cost there is one extra encode of `messages + systemPrompt + prompt`. Loop-hook iterations add one encode per iteration. Not measured separately.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - `assembleHarnessContextEngine` passes `currentTokenCount` to the engine when supplied.
  - `assembleHarnessContextEngine` omits the field when caller does not supply it (so engines can detect older runtimes via `params.currentTokenCount === undefined`).
  - `installContextEngineLoopHook` reads `currentTokenCount` from `getRuntimeContext()` when the callback returns it; omits otherwise; omits when no callback is wired.
  - The production loop-hook install site at `attempt.ts:1996` computes `currentTokenCount` and feeds it into the `getRuntimeContext` runtime context (asserted by capturing the callback in `attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`).
  - The attempt-level `assembleAttemptContextEngine` re-export still plumbs the field.
  - The Codex harness call at `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts:624` computes a positive `currentTokenCount` and passes it through (asserted in `run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`).
- **Edge cases checked:**
  - Missing `getRuntimeContext` callback in the loop hook → field omitted.
  - `getRuntimeContext` returns an object without `currentTokenCount` → field omitted.
  - Older callers that don't supply `currentTokenCount` → unchanged behavior.
- **What you did NOT verify:**
  - End-to-end run against a real LLM provider where pre-this-PR overflow would have occurred. This is the most important real-world signal and will be performed before merge.
  - Performance impact of the additional `estimatePrePromptTokens` calls under stress (large transcripts, fast tool loops).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A. The new field is optional. Existing context engines compile and run unchanged. Engines that want to opt in simply read `params.currentTokenCount` and gate behavior on whether it is present.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** The additional `estimatePrePromptTokens` call at each main assemble call site adds one extra token-estimation pass per turn, plus one per tool-loop iteration on the PI loop-hook path.
  - **Mitigation:** Uses the same helper the runtime already invokes at the preemptive-compaction precheck on the same turn (PI main path), so the marginal cost there is small and amortized. Loop-hook iterations add one encode per iteration; if this proves measurably costly under sustained tool loops, the result can be cached or computed lazily.
- **Risk:** Engines that misread the new field (e.g. treat absent as zero) could over-restrict their injection on older runtimes that don't pass the field.
  - **Mitigation:** Field is explicitly **omitted** rather than set to `undefined` or `0` when not supplied (see the `...(typeof x === "number" ? { x } : {})` spread idiom in the wrappers). Engines that follow the documented contract (`params.currentTokenCount === undefined` ⇒ no headroom info available) behave correctly.
- **Risk:** Re-exporting `estimatePrePromptTokens` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime` widens the public extension API by one function and commits us to maintaining its signature.
  - **Mitigation:** The function is small, pure, and stable — it already exists at its source location and is used internally. Re-exporting just makes the same shape available to extension harnesses. If the internal signature ever needs to change, the barrel re-export can be wrapped at that point.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): per-group allowFrom for sender authorization
target.body:
Fixes #69926.

Brings WhatsApp to parity with Feishu, IRC, LINE, Telegram, and Nextcloud-talk by accepting a per-group `allowFrom` list on `channels.whatsapp.groups.<jid>` (and the matching account-scoped entry). A non-empty per-group `allowFrom` overrides (does not merge with) the channel/account-level `groupAllowFrom` for that one JID, while groups without an override keep the channel/account-level list. Empty arrays fall back to the channel/account-level allowlist.

ClawSweeper triage explicitly cleared this as "narrow, source-proven feature gap with clear files, tests, and boundaries."

## Change

- `WhatsAppGroupEntrySchema` and `WhatsAppGroupConfig` gain an optional `allowFrom: string[]` (E.164 phone numbers).
- New `resolveWhatsAppConversationGroupAllowFrom` resolver in `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound-policy.ts`, wired into `resolveWhatsAppIngressAccess` so the effective `groupAllowFrom` passed to `resolveStableChannelMessageIngress` reflects the per-group override for group conversations.
- The resolver respects the same multi-account inheritance guard as the rest of the WhatsApp config: account-scoped `groups[<jid>].allowFrom` takes precedence over a root-scope entry for the same JID, so root groups do not silently leak into accounts with their own groups map.
- `bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts` regenerated to surface the new field in the bundled channel schema (verified via `pnpm config:channels:check`).
- `docs/channels/whatsapp.md` documents the per-group override semantics and shows a mixed-trust example.

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound-policy.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts` -- 17/17 pass (6 new resolver cases + 2 new schema cases + existing schema tests).
- `pnpm config:channels:check` clean.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` clean on every touched file.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: operators should be able to express different sender allowlists per WhatsApp group (e.g. "Group A: any team member can drive the bot; Group B: only staff; Group C: only me") without dropping back to a single channel-wide allowlist. The runtime resolver should also honor the multi-account inheritance guard so root-scope per-group lists do not leak into accounts with their own groups map.

Real environment tested: local Linux 6.17 (Node 22.22.2). Live Node process driving the **real production** `resolveWhatsAppInboundPolicy` and `resolveWhatsAppConversationGroupAllowFrom` helpers from this PR (no mocks). The proof feeds the helper realistic OpenClaw configs (single-account, multi-account, override+inherit+empty cases) and prints the effective `groupAllowFrom` the inbound gate would apply.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
node --import tsx /tmp/proof-69926.mts
```

Where `/tmp/proof-69926.mts` directly imports the production resolver helpers from `extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound-policy.ts` and validates per-group override, fallback on empty, account-scoped override, and inheritance behavior across seven scenarios.

Evidence after fix: terminal output captured directly from the live Node process exercising the production helpers.

```
--- channel-level only (single-account) ---
  group with per-group allowFrom override
    -> ["+15550003333","+15550004444"]
  group with a different per-group allowFrom
    -> ["+15550005555"]
  group WITHOUT override -> inherits channel-level
    -> ["+15550001111"]
  empty per-group allowFrom -> inherits channel-level
    -> ["+15550001111"]

--- multi-account: account-scoped groups override account-level groupAllowFrom ---
  work account, group has per-group override
    -> ["+15550007777"]
  work account, no override on this jid -> inherits work groupAllowFrom
    -> ["+15550008888"]
  personal account, no groups map at all -> inherits personal groupAllowFrom
    -> ["+15550006666"]
```

Observed result after fix:

- Group with a per-group `allowFrom` returns exactly that list, suppressing the channel-level `groupAllowFrom` for that JID.
- A different per-group entry returns its own list independently -- the override is per-JID, not channel-wide.
- A group without a per-group entry returns the channel-level `groupAllowFrom`, so existing configs keep working unchanged.
- An empty `allowFrom: []` falls back to the channel-level list, so accidental empties do not silently fail closed for the group.
- In a multi-account config, the work account's per-group override applies for that JID, and groups without overrides correctly inherit the work account's `groupAllowFrom` rather than leaking from the personal account or the channel root.
- The personal account, which has no `groups` map of its own, falls back to its own account-level `groupAllowFrom`.

What was not tested: a live WhatsApp message ingress against a real WhatsApp Web account. The runtime proof above exercises the production resolver and `resolveWhatsAppInboundPolicy` chain, and the unit suite covers the schema acceptance plus the resolver semantics under every documented case, but neither runs a real inbound WhatsApp event against a paired account.

target.title: feat(memory-core): Memory Privacy Audit + Encrypted Backup
target.body:
## Summary
Build the first contribution as **privacy visibility plus encrypted backup**, not live encrypted memory. The feature adds a safe CLI workflow that audits where OpenClaw stores memory-related data, flags privacy risks, and supports encrypted export/import of memory artifacts.

### Key Changes
- Add CLI commands:
  - `openclaw memory privacy audit`
  - `openclaw memory export --encrypted --out <file>.age`
  - `openclaw memory import --encrypted --in <file>.age --target <dir>`
- Audit reports:
  - memory workspace path
  - `MEMORY.md`, `memory/*.md`, `DREAMS.md`, `memory/.dreams/`, and dreaming reports
  - SQLite/vector index path and size
  - embedding provider classification: local, remote, none, or unknown
  - Active Memory transcript persistence state
  - plaintext memory surfaces and backup risks
- Encrypted backup:
  - export creates a tar archive of selected memory artifacts, then encrypts it
  - output uses the `.age` format via the TypeScript `age-encryption` package
  - import decrypts into an explicit target directory
  - import refuses to overwrite existing files by default
  - no live memory files are changed during export

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Adds memory privacy visibility plus encrypted backup/restore at rest. This proof covers `openclaw memory privacy audit`, non-empty `openclaw memory export --encrypted`, and `openclaw memory import --encrypted` after removing dependency-graph changes and fixing current PR checks in commit `75714827f7`.

**Real environment tested:** Isolated local temp lab only: `<redacted-local-proof-root>`, with `OPENCLAW_HOME` pointed at `<redacted-local-proof-root>/home`. The normal production OpenClaw home was not used. The temp proof directory was removed immediately after the run.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```console
$ export OPENCLAW_HOME=<redacted-local-proof-root>/home
$ export OPENCLAW_MEMORY_BACKUP_PASSPHRASE='non-secret-proof-passphrase'
$ node openclaw.mjs memory privacy audit
$ node openclaw.mjs memory export --encrypted --out <redacted-local-proof-root>/memory-backup.age
$ node openclaw.mjs memory import --encrypted --in <redacted-local-proof-root>/memory-backup.age --target <redacted-local-proof-root>/restore
$ shasum -a 256 \
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/MEMORY.md <redacted-local-proof-root>/restore/MEMORY.md \
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/DREAMS.md <redacted-local-proof-root>/restore/DREAMS.md \
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/memory/2026-05-29.md <redacted-local-proof-root>/restore/memory/2026-05-29.md
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the isolated lab run:
```console
commit=75714827f7
PROOF_ROOT=<redacted-local-proof-root>
OPENCLAW_HOME=<redacted-local-proof-root>/home
--- audit ---
Memory Privacy (main)
Workspace: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace
Embeddings: openai (remote)
Session transcripts: not found (0 files)
Artifacts:
  memory-file · present · 1 files · 13 B
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/MEMORY.md
  memory-directory · present · 1 files · 19 B
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/memory
  dreaming-artifact · present · 1 files · 12 B
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/DREAMS.md
  dreaming-artifact · missing
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/memory/.dreams
  index · present · 1 files · 8.0 KiB
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite
  sessions · missing
    $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions
Findings:
  warning remote-embedding-provider Memory embeddings use remote provider "openai"; memory text may leave this machine during indexing/search.
  warning plaintext-memory-artifacts Memory, dreaming, or session artifacts are stored as readable local files.
  warning memory-index-present A memory index exists on disk and may reveal keywords or semantic metadata.
  action encrypted-backup-available Use `openclaw memory export --encrypted` to create an encrypted backup before moving or archiving memory.

--- export ---
Encrypted memory backup written: <redacted-local-proof-root>/memory-backup.age (3 files)
--- import ---
Encrypted memory backup restored: <redacted-local-proof-root>/restore (3 files)
--- restored sha256 ---
7478557d49e64bc7bab77fbf78eda7a6ca5e0c1d73999ae887cf860efd9efb67  $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/MEMORY.md
7478557d49e64bc7bab77fbf78eda7a6ca5e0c1d73999ae887cf860efd9efb67  <redacted-local-proof-root>/restore/MEMORY.md
7ed6c1b91736edbabe911206e16b0a4a1346f1b839af80dbaa982f17f3b58664  $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/DREAMS.md
7ed6c1b91736edbabe911206e16b0a4a1346f1b839af80dbaa982f17f3b58664  <redacted-local-proof-root>/restore/DREAMS.md
8f1fdb6b274414d6c214466cc418d68072ecc3eea9be8d5605c4af327fb60638  $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/workspace/memory/2026-05-29.md
8f1fdb6b274414d6c214466cc418d68072ecc3eea9be8d5605c4af327fb60638  <redacted-local-proof-root>/restore/memory/2026-05-29.md

<redacted-local-proof-root>/restore/DREAMS.md
<redacted-local-proof-root>/restore/MEMORY.md
<redacted-local-proof-root>/restore/memory/2026-05-29.md
```

**Observed result after fix:** The audit detected the non-empty lab memory artifacts and warned about plaintext memory/index surfaces and the configured remote embedding provider. The encrypted export wrote a backup containing 3 files. The encrypted import restored 3 files, and SHA256 hashes matched between source and restored `MEMORY.md`, `DREAMS.md`, and `memory/2026-05-29.md`. The isolated proof directory was cleaned up after the run.

**What was not tested:** Live encrypted memory is intentionally out of scope for this phase. I did not test the production OpenClaw home; all proof used the isolated temp lab above.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): registerChatStreamRenderer for plugin-owned inline UI
target.body:
This PR adds a narrow SDK/Gateway seam for plugin-owned inline chat-stream UI without making plugins mutate core chat rendering directly. Reviewers can treat it as a descriptor/projection feature: plugins register chat-stream renderers, the Gateway projects ordered descriptors, and clients decide how to render them.

## Summary

This PR adds the missing OpenClaw plugin SDK seam for plugin-owned inline chat-stream UI. The important design boundary is that plugins declare metadata only; they do not ship rendering code into the sandbox. The host/Gateway projects typed descriptors, and compatible clients decide how to render surfaces such as inline plan cards, input-bar ownership, or input-toolbar chips.

- Problem: current Control UI descriptors cover static surfaces only (`session`, `tool`, `run`, `settings`), so plugins cannot express inline chat-stream UI without core rendering changes.
- Why it matters: plugin features like plan approval cards need a first-class, declarative host seam instead of bespoke core UI patches.
- What changed: adds `chat-message`, `chat-input-bar`, and `chat-input-toolbar-chip` descriptor surfaces, plus optional `priority` and chat-only `activeWhen` metadata, Gateway projection ordering, docs, and Swift protocol bindings.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no plugin-rendered JS/React crosses into core; the seam is metadata/projection only, and static descriptor slots remain backward compatible.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related electricsheephq/Smarter-Claw#78
- Related electricsheephq/Smarter-Claw#77
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## What's Added

| Surface | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `chat-message` | Plugin-owned widget rendered between chat messages, such as an inline plan-approval card. |
| `chat-input-bar` | Plugin-owned contribution to the chat input bar, such as owning the composer while approval is pending. |
| `chat-input-toolbar-chip` | Plugin-owned chip rendered in the chat input toolbar, such as an active mode indicator. |

Two optional descriptor fields are added:

- `priority?: number`: deterministic sort key among multiple descriptors on the same surface.
- `activeWhen?: { sessionExtensionNamespace: string; valuePath?: string[]; equals?: JsonValue }`: chat-stream-only predicate evaluated by compatible clients against the existing session-extension projection.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: plugin-authored chat-stream Control UI descriptors need to survive contract validation and appear in Gateway `plugins.uiDescriptors` output with deterministic ordering and attached plugin metadata.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-1`, branch `feat/plugin-sdk-chat-stream-renderer`, latest patch `50d2d25d265`, exercising plugin contract validation plus Gateway descriptor projection tests. This is OpenClaw contract/Gateway proof, not a browser-rendered consumer plugin run.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts
pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/plugins/host-hooks.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts --format default
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/plugins/host-hooks.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts docs/plugins/hooks.md docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md
git diff --check
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 5.41s
Contract shard: Test Files 1 passed, Tests 54 passed
Gateway shard: Test Files 1 passed, Tests 4 passed

oxlint: Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
oxfmt: All matched files use the correct format.
git diff --check: passed
```

Gateway projection test emits client-visible descriptor rows equivalent to:

```json
[
  { "pluginId": "m-plugin", "id": "negative", "surface": "chat-message", "priority": -5 },
  { "pluginId": "a-plugin", "id": "card-1", "surface": "chat-message", "priority": 10 },
  { "pluginId": "a-plugin", "id": "card-2", "surface": "chat-message", "priority": 10 },
  { "pluginId": "z-plugin", "id": "card-1", "surface": "chat-message", "priority": 50 },
  { "pluginId": "m-plugin", "id": "no-priority", "surface": "chat-input-toolbar-chip" }
]
```

- Observed result after fix: chat-stream descriptors validate, malformed `priority`/`activeWhen` values are rejected, `activeWhen` is rejected on static surfaces, `plugins.uiDescriptors` returns deterministic priority/plugin/id ordering, and docs now list the chat-stream surfaces in both hooks and SDK overview docs.
- What was not tested: a browser-rendered Smarter-Claw plan card. That consumer is intentionally blocked on this upstream SDK seam landing.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): ClawSweeper found `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md` still listed only the four static surfaces; patch `50d2d25d265` updates that public overview.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A. This is a new SDK/Gateway capability, not a regression in existing behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: chat-stream descriptor validation, static-surface rejection for `activeWhen`, malformed metadata rejection, deterministic Gateway projection ordering.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the API seam is metadata validation/projection, so contract and Gateway handler tests cover the core behavior without a consumer UI.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A, tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Plugin authors gain declarative chat-stream Control UI descriptor surfaces. Existing clients/plugins that do not understand the new surfaces can ignore them; existing static descriptor behavior remains unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

<!-- reviewer-map -->

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  plugin["Plugin registers chat-stream renderer"] --> host["Plugin host descriptor registry"]
  host --> gateway["Gateway projection"]
  gateway --> client["Control UI / Swift client descriptor"]
  client --> render["Plugin-owned inline chat UI"]
  plugin -. "no direct core chat mutation" .-> render
```

```text
[Plugin registers descriptor metadata]
  -> [Plugin registry validates surface, priority, activeWhen]
  -> [Gateway plugins.uiDescriptors projects sorted rows]
  -> [Compatible client renders chat-stream UI]

No plugin rendering code crosses the sandbox boundary.
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A. The PR adds descriptor metadata and projection only.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local development host
- Runtime/container: local Lexar-backed OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: not model-provider dependent
- Integration/channel (if any): plugin SDK contract and Gateway `plugins.uiDescriptors` handler
- Relevant config (redacted): none

### Steps

1. Run contract and Gateway descriptor tests.
2. Run targeted lint and formatter checks.
3. Verify docs mention all chat-stream descriptor surfaces.

### Expected

- Descriptor validation and Gateway projection tests pass.
- SDK docs list chat-stream surfaces.
- No lint/format failures.

### Actual

- 58 focused tests passed.
- Targeted lint, formatter, and whitespace checks passed.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: contract validation, malformed metadata rejection, static-surface `activeWhen` rejection, Gateway projection order, plugin metadata attachment, docs wording.
- Edge cases checked: negative priority, missing priority, plugin/id tie-breakers, docs `chat-input-toolbar-chip` wording.
- What you did **not** verify: rendered browser consumer UI and full GitHub CI matrix.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

ClawSweeper P3 addressed by `50d2d25d265`: update the SDK overview surface list. Prior lint/doc cleanup is included in remote commits `3df8c394d72`, `34e7ba5ec12`, and `4f6f0f71c58`.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: this is a new public SDK/Gateway seam and needs maintainer API-shape approval.
  - Mitigation: the seam is declarative metadata only, rejects malformed descriptors before projection, and preserves client-owned rendering.

target.title: fix(cli): differentiate gateway-restart hint for hot-loadable agent config sets (#80722)
target.body:
## Summary

- `config set`, `config patch`, and `config unset` now derive their post-write hint from the shared gateway reload plan instead of always printing `Restart the gateway to apply.`
- Hot-reloadable config paths, including `agents.list.*`, `agents.defaults.models.*`, and `models.*` runtime-policy edits, now print `Change will apply without restarting the gateway.`
- Restart-required paths still keep `Restart the gateway to apply.`; mixed batches keep the restart hint if any touched path requires restart.
- Legacy whole-agent runtime keys such as `agents.list[].agentRuntime` no longer force a restart hint, matching current runtime-selection docs/source where those keys are ignored by runtime selection.

Closes #80722.

## Why

The old CLI success text was stale after reload metadata landed: `agents.list` and model config are classified as hot-reloadable by the gateway reload plan, but CLI writes still printed the generic restart warning. That made active agent/model changes look restart-required even when they apply without a gateway restart.

The earlier implementation treated legacy per-agent `agentRuntime.id` as load-bearing. Current main has moved runtime selection to provider/model policy, so this refresh aligns the CLI hint with `buildGatewayReloadPlan()` instead of encoding legacy whole-agent runtime semantics in the CLI.

## Implementation

- Added `configApplyHintForPaths()` / `configApplyHintForOperations()` in `src/cli/config-cli.ts`.
- The helper calls `buildGatewayReloadPlan()` with the changed config paths.
- Result mapping:
  - `restartGateway: true` -> `Restart the gateway to apply.`
  - hot reasons present -> `Change will apply without restarting the gateway.`
  - no hot/restart reason -> `No gateway restart needed.`
- `runConfigOperations()` and direct `runConfigUnset()` use the helper for success text.
- Added regression coverage for active agent model writes, legacy whole-agent `agentRuntime`, provider runtime policy, restart-required gateway paths, and mixed hot+restart batches.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The CLI used to print `Restart the gateway to apply.` after hot-reloadable config writes such as active agent model updates. After this patch, the CLI prints the hot-reload hint for hot paths while keeping the restart hint for restart-required paths.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `0b18f14e4b7fa699f82eae9a98a3a6e81ca828a4`, using the repository's real `node scripts/run-node.mjs config` command against a temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json` fixture.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'agents.list[0].model.primary' '"openai/gpt-5.4-mini"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'agents.list[1].model.primary' '"openai/gpt-5.4-mini"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'models.providers.openai.agentRuntime.id' '"pi"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'gateway.auth.mode' 'token'
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config unset 'agents.list[1].agentRuntime'
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from those live CLI commands:

```console
Updated agents.list.0.model.primary. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated agents.list.1.model.primary. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated models.providers.openai.agentRuntime.id. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated gateway.auth.mode. Restart the gateway to apply.
Removed agents.list[1].agentRuntime. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Active-agent model writes, legacy whole-agent runtime removal, and provider runtime-policy edits now avoid the stale restart hint; the restart-required gateway auth path still prints `Restart the gateway to apply.`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a full long-lived gateway reload loop here; this PR only changes the CLI post-write success hint and reuses the existing gateway reload planner for the classification.

### Current-head proof update (2026-06-20)

After the latest direct-unset change, I verified the broad root removals that ClawSweeper called out on head `01e96400a197`.

`config unset models` with `models.pricing.enabled` present:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-80823-proof-models.HdGPd1/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80823-proof-models.HdGPd1/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_NO_COLOR=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs config unset models
Removed models. Restart the gateway to apply.
```

`config unset plugins` with `plugins.load.paths` present:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-80823-proof-plugins.gWf5XB/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80823-proof-plugins.gWf5XB/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_NO_COLOR=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs config unset plugins
Removed plugins. Restart the gateway to apply.
```

The first live proof attempt exposed the root cause: whole-object removals collapsed to `models` / `plugins` before reload planning, so restart-owned descendants such as `models.pricing.*` and `plugins.load.*` could be hidden. The latest patch expands whole-object add/remove diffs to their descendant paths before calling `buildGatewayReloadPlan()`; config write behavior is unchanged.


## Verification

```console
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/cli/config-cli.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts --threads=1
Finished in 22ms on 2 files using 1 threads.

$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  105 passed (105)

$ git diff --check
# exit 0
```

## Compatibility / risk

Low behavior risk: this only changes CLI success text after config writes. It does not change config parsing, validation, file writes, reload behavior, or gateway runtime selection. The hint now follows the same reload planner used by the gateway, so future reload-rule changes are not duplicated in the CLI.



## Current-head refresh, 2026-06-21

Updated this branch against current `main` to clear the red CI paths on head `da00cc2d36be5599ef9338f95123bdd6e1eb4fe1`. The PR-visible diff after the merge is still limited to:

- `src/cli/config-cli.ts`
- `src/cli/config-cli.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts`

Verification on head `da00cc2d36be5599ef9338f95123bdd6e1eb4fe1`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts
PASS: 1 file, 126 tests
```

target.title: fix(/v1/responses): accept text field on requests for OpenAI SDK 6.x parity
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `/v1/responses` rejects every request from clients using the current OpenAI Node SDK (`openai` >= 6.x) or `@langchain/openai` >= 1.x with `Bad request - please check your parameters: Unrecognized key: "text"`. The OpenAI SDK auto-populates a top-level `text: { format: { type: "text" } }` field on every Responses-API request, but `CreateResponseBodySchema` is `.strict()` and does not include `text`.
- Why it matters: this breaks n8n's AI Agent (Tools Agent V3) and any other downstream client using a current OpenAI SDK against OpenClaw's "OpenAI-compatible" Responses endpoint. The endpoint is effectively unusable from those clients today.
- What changed: added `text` to `CreateResponseBodySchema` with an OpenAI-spec-shaped object — `format` is a discriminated union over `text` / `json_schema` / `json_object`, plus optional `verbosity`. Schema acceptance only; no runtime behavior change.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): `openresponses-http.ts` agent dispatch, prompt build, or output handling. Only the request schema. `format.type === "json_schema"` and `"json_object"` parse successfully but are not yet enforced at runtime; that belongs in a follow-up PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `/v1/responses` returns HTTP 400 `Unrecognized key: "text"` when called by any current OpenAI Node SDK (`openai` 6.37.0 in this case, via `@langchain/openai` 1.1.3 inside n8n 2.19.5).
- Real environment tested: macOS 15 (Darwin 25.4.0), OpenClaw `2026.5.4` running as the dedicated `openclaw` LaunchDaemon, gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789` with `gateway.auth.mode: token` and `gateway.http.endpoints.responses.enabled: true`. n8n 2.19.5 self-hosted via `npx n8n`, AI Agent node `@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.agent` v3.1.
- Exact steps or command run: enabled the `responses` endpoint, configured an n8n OpenAI credential against `http://localhost:18789/v1` with the gateway bearer token, wired an AI Agent (Tools Agent V3) with model `openclaw`, executed the workflow.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture from this Mac):

Before (live, against the production-installed OpenClaw on this machine):

```
$ curl -s -X POST http://localhost:18789/v1/responses \
    -H \"authorization: Bearer \$TOKEN\" -H \"content-type: application/json\" \
    -d '{\"model\":\"openclaw\",\"input\":\"hi\",\"text\":{\"format\":{\"type\":\"text\"}}}' \
    -w \"\\nHTTP %{http_code}\\n\"
{\"error\":{\"message\":\": Unrecognized key: \\\"text\\\"\",\"type\":\"invalid_request_error\"}}
HTTP 400
```

n8n surfaces the same as a `LangChain MODEL_NOT_FOUND` (404 wrapper) on the AI Agent node:

```
NodeOperationError: The resource you are requesting could not be found
  at executeBatch (.../@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain/.../ToolsAgent/V3/helpers/executeBatch.ts:113:11)
  at ExecuteContext.toolsAgentExecute (.../ToolsAgent/V3/execute.ts:46:66)
```

After (patched schema parsed in isolation against the same request bodies a current OpenAI SDK / n8n sends):

```
$ node --experimental-strip-types -e \"...import patched schema...\"
ACCEPT   auto-populated default (n8n / openai SDK 6.x)
ACCEPT   json_schema structured output
ACCEPT   verbosity only
REJECT   invalid format type
           Invalid discriminator value. Expected 'text' | 'json_schema' | 'json_object'
REJECT   unknown top-level field (regression check)
           Unrecognized key: \"notARealField\"
```

- Observed result after fix: requests that previously 400'd at the schema layer now parse successfully; the strict-validation contract on the rest of the body is preserved (still rejects unknown top-level keys and invalid format types).
- What was not tested: full n8n round-trip against the patched gateway. The patched OpenClaw build was validated via the schema in isolation; swapping the deployed npm-bundled `openresponses-http-*.js` on the live install and re-running the n8n workflow is the next verification step but requires replacing a globally-installed bundled file (out of scope for this PR's evidence). Unit + parity tests cover the schema contract.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `CreateResponseBodySchema` declared `.strict()` and the `text` field was not in its allowed-keys list. OpenAI's Responses API spec defines `text.format` (and a newer `text.verbosity`), and the `openai` Node SDK >= 6.x auto-populates `text: { format: { type: \"text\" } }` on every request even when no structured output is requested. The strict schema rejects that auto-populated field, returning HTTP 400 before the request reaches the agent runtime.
- Missing detection / guardrail: parity tests only covered fields previously sent by Codex / direct curl probes; no test exercised the request shape produced by an unmodified `openai` SDK or `@langchain/openai` instance at the versions n8n ships with.
- Contributing context: the existing schema already has a documented \"Phase 1: ignore but accept these fields\" group (`temperature`, `top_p`, `metadata`, `store`, `previous_response_id`, `reasoning`, `truncation`). `text` belongs in the same accept-but-don't-yet-honor bucket for OpenAI-SDK parity.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/open-responses.schema.test.ts` (new, colocated). 10 cases pinning the `text` contract: default `text` accepted, `json_schema` accepted, `json_object` accepted, `verbosity` only, empty `text`, unknown `format.type` rejected, `json_schema` missing required fields rejected, unknown top-level keys on `text` rejected, plus a regression case asserting the rest of `CreateResponseBodySchema` stays strict, and a full-shape happy path matching what an OpenAI SDK 6.x client sends today.
- Scenario the test should lock in: the gateway must accept the request body shape any current OpenAI SDK / LangChain ChatOpenAI 1.x emits, while still rejecting unknown top-level fields and malformed `text.format` payloads.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression is a schema-level contract violation; a colocated zod-schema unit test is the cheapest place to catch it. A live HTTP test would also catch it but adds gateway boot, auth, and agent setup cost for no extra signal.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none. `openresponses-parity.test.ts` covers `input_image`, `input_file`, and tool-definition shape but not the OpenAI-SDK-emitted `text` field.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Requests carrying `text` (any OpenAI-SDK >= 6.x or `@langchain/openai` >= 1.x client) no longer fail with HTTP 400 at the schema layer.
- Default `text.format.type === \"text\"` is the only format honored at runtime; no change to current default-text output behavior.
- `text.format.type === \"json_schema\"` and `\"json_object\"` are accepted by the schema but silently produce regular text output; runtime enforcement is intentionally left for a follow-up since it requires changes to the agent dispatch path.
- No new defaults, no config keys.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A — schema-only change.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

The change is purely a request-validation widening on an already-authenticated, already-feature-flagged endpoint (`gateway.http.endpoints.responses.enabled`). No new fields are persisted, logged, or forwarded; non-text formats are silently treated as text.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15 (Darwin 25.4.0)
- Runtime/container: native macOS install via `npm i -g openclaw` (LaunchDaemon as user `openclaw`)
- Model/provider: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` (default agent), accessed via the `openclaw` model id over the `/v1/responses` endpoint
- Integration/channel: HTTP via `n8n` 2.19.5 AI Agent (Tools Agent V3) using `@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain` 3.1, `@langchain/openai` 1.1.3, `openai` 6.37.0
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json
{
  \"gateway\": {
    \"auth\": { \"mode\": \"token\", \"token\": \"<48-char redacted>\" },
    \"http\": {
      \"endpoints\": {
        \"chatCompletions\": { \"enabled\": true },
        \"responses\":       { \"enabled\": true }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Enable `gateway.http.endpoints.responses.enabled: true` and restart the gateway.
2. `curl` the endpoint with the SDK-default body shape:
   `curl -X POST http://localhost:18789/v1/responses -H 'authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{\"model\":\"openclaw\",\"input\":\"hi\",\"text\":{\"format\":{\"type\":\"text\"}}}'`

### Expected

- HTTP 200 with a Responses-shaped JSON body (`object: \"response\"`, `output[].content[].text`).

### Actual (before this PR)

- HTTP 400 with `{\"error\":{\"message\":\": Unrecognized key: \\\"text\\\"\",\"type\":\"invalid_request_error\"}}`. n8n surfaces this as a LangChain `MODEL_NOT_FOUND` 404 on the AI Agent node and the workflow halts.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (terminal capture above)
- [x] Trace/log snippets (n8n stack trace above)
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm test src/gateway/open-responses.schema.test.ts` — 10/10 pass.
  - `pnpm test src/gateway/openresponses-parity.test.ts src/gateway/openresponses-phase.test.ts src/gateway/open-responses.schema.test.ts` — 34/34 pass.
  - `pnpm check:changed` — green (lint, runtime sidecar guard, import cycles, webhook/auth lints).
  - `pnpm check:test-types` — green (`tsgo:core:test` and `tsgo:extensions:test`).
  - `pnpm format:check` on every changed file — green (auto-formatted with `oxfmt`).
  - `pnpm build` — clean.
  - `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — green.
  - Schema parity proof: parsed the SDK-default request body, a `json_schema` request, a `verbosity`-only request, an invalid `format.type`, and an unknown top-level key against the patched schema; first three accepted, last two rejected with clear messages.
- Edge cases checked: empty `text` object, `verbosity` without `format`, malformed `json_schema` (missing `name`/`schema`), unknown top-level key on `text`, unknown top-level key on the request (regression check that the rest of the schema stays `.strict()`).
- What I did **not** verify:
  - End-to-end n8n round-trip against a swapped-in patched gateway build. Doing that requires replacing the bundled `openresponses-http-*.js` in a global npm install; I left it for a follow-up rather than tampering with the user's running OpenClaw install.
  - Runtime enforcement of `text.format.type === \"json_schema\" | \"json_object\"` (intentionally out of scope; requires agent-dispatch changes and likely a separate design discussion).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — strictly widens what the schema accepts; nothing previously accepted is now rejected.
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: clients that send `text.format.type === \"json_schema\"` may now expect structured-output enforcement that the runtime does not yet provide, and silently receive plain text.
  - Mitigation: documented this in the schema comment and in CHANGELOG so the limitation is discoverable; runtime enforcement can be added in a follow-up PR without further schema changes. The default OpenAI SDK auto-populated case (`text.format.type === \"text\"`) is the dominant real-world request shape today and is fully honored.

commands.author: ml12580

target.title: fix(cli): accept parent options placed after lazy subcommands (#55563 regression 1) [AI-assisted]
target.body:
> [AI-assisted] This PR was generated using Claude Code.

## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw browser tabs --browser-profile remote` (parent option placed *after* the subcommand) fails with `error: unknown option '--browser-profile'`. The pre-v2026.3.24 syntax `openclaw browser --browser-profile remote tabs` still works, so this is a silent regression of the option-ordering flexibility that existing WSL2/CDP documentation relies on. Regression 1 of #55563.
- **Why it matters now:** Every user following the existing browser/CDP troubleshooting docs gets an error that looks like a config problem, not a CLI syntax change. Reported by a contributor whose merged PR #39407 still documents the old form.
- **Intended outcome:** A parent option placed after a lazy subcommand is accepted again, matching the pre-lazy behavior and the existing root-option normalization (`--no-color`, `--log-level` after subcommands).
- **Out of scope:** Regression 2 of #55563 (gateway cycling after `openclaw doctor --fix`) is a separate code path already tracked by open PR #94148; this PR does not touch `doctor`.
- **Success look:** `openclaw browser tabs --browser-profile remote` parses and reaches the `tabs` action with `browserProfile === "remote"`, identical to `openclaw browser --browser-profile remote tabs`.
- **Reviewer focus:** `hoistParentOptionsBeforeSubcommand` in `src/cli/program/helpers.ts` and its single call site in `src/cli/program/action-reparse.ts`. The hoist only moves tokens that match a parent/ancestor command's own option flags from after the subcommand to before it; it is a no-op when parent options already precede the subcommand, so working invocations are unchanged.

## Linked context

Addresses Regression 1 of #55563 (the issue bundles two regressions; only the browser CLI syntax break is fixed here).

Related #55563 (Regression 2 — `doctor --fix` gateway cycling — tracked separately by #94148). Also related to the prior root-option-after-subcommand fixes #93455 (`--log-level`) and the `--no-color` normalization, which established the argv-normalization pattern this extends to parent-command options.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw browser tabs --browser-profile remote` rejected `--browser-profile` as an unknown option because the lazy reparse replays the original argv verbatim and the real `tabs` subcommand does not define (or allow) the parent's `--browser-profile`.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.2.2, OpenClaw built from source on branch `fix/vuln-55563`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the real CLI `node ./openclaw.mjs browser tabs --browser-profile remote` and the lazy-reparse integration test in `extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts` (real Commander parse + real lazy reparse; only the browser network modules are stubbed, the option-parsing path under fix is real).
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):
```
$ node ./openclaw.mjs browser tabs --browser-profile remote   (real CLI, fix applied)
(no "unknown option" error — parse accepts --browser-profile after the subcommand;
 command proceeds into the tabs action. No browser gateway is running in this env,
 so it does not list tabs, but the option-ordering regression is resolved.)

$ pnpm test -- extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts   (fix applied)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
  accepts --browser-profile placed after the lazy subcommand (#55563 regression 1)
    -> tabsAction called once; browser parent opts browserProfile === "remote"
```
- Observed result after fix: The parent option `--browser-profile remote` placed after the `tabs` subcommand is accepted; the `tabs` action runs with `browserProfile === "remote"`, identical to placing the option before the subcommand. A bare `--browser-profile` with no following value (or followed by another flag like `--json`) is left in place, so Commander still reports the missing-value/unknown-option error and the `tabs` action does not run — the bare flag is never hoisted alone (which would let Commander consume the next token as the value).
```
$ node ./openclaw.mjs browser tabs --browser-profile   (real CLI, fix applied)
error: unknown option '--browser-profile'
(missing-value path preserved: bare parent value option is NOT hoisted alone,
 so the subcommand name is never swallowed as the option value)

$ pnpm test -- src/cli/program/helpers.test.ts extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts   (fix applied)
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  57 passed (57)
  ✓ does not hoist a bare value option with no following value
  ✓ does not hoist a value option followed by another flag
  ✓ does not hoist an empty --flag= form
  ✓ preserves the missing-value error for a bare --browser-profile after the subcommand (#55563 regression 1)
  ✓ preserves the missing-value error when --browser-profile is followed by another flag (#55563 regression 1)
```
- What was not tested: macOS/Linux, non-browser lazy command trees with deeply nested subcommands beyond the two-level `browser tab new` shape, and the actual tab listing against a live browser gateway (no browser running in this environment).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The before/after contrast is captured through the real lazy-reparse path exercised by the integration test (real Commander + real reparse + real hoist; browser network modules stubbed) and corroborated by the real `openclaw` CLI accepting the option. A live browser gateway was not available locally.
- Before evidence:
```
$ pnpm test -- extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts   (fix reverted, HEAD~1)
error: unknown option '--browser-profile'
 |extension-browser| extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts (8 tests | 1 failed)
  x accepts --browser-profile placed after the lazy subcommand (#55563 regression 1)
Error: process.exit unexpectedly called with "1"
  Command.error node_modules/commander/lib/command.js:1957:10
 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 7 passed (8)
```

## Tests and validation

- Commands run: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` (changed files clean), `pnpm tsgo` (exit 0), `pnpm exec oxlint --type-aware` on changed files (exit 0), `pnpm build` (exit 0, including `write-cli-startup-metadata` postbuild), `pnpm test -- src/cli/program/helpers.test.ts src/cli/program/action-reparse.test.ts` (47 + 6 pass), `pnpm test -- extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.lazy.test.ts` (10 pass).
- Regression coverage added: `hoistParentOptionsBeforeSubcommand` unit tests (`helpers.test.ts`, 11 cases: value/boolean hoist, `--flag=value`, no-op when already before, subcommand-own-option preserved, root-option skip, root-parent no-op, subcommand-not-found no-op, plus 3 missing-value preservation cases — bare value option, value option followed by another flag, empty `--flag=` form); an `action-reparse.test.ts` case asserting the hoisted argv is re-parsed; and `browser-cli.lazy.test.ts` cases asserting `browser tabs --browser-profile remote` reaches the `tabs` action with `browserProfile === "remote"` (fails on `HEAD~1` with `unknown option '--browser-profile'`) and that bare `browser tabs --browser-profile` / `browser tabs --browser-profile --json` preserve the missing-value error (the `tabs` action does not run).
- What failed before this fix: `openclaw browser tabs --browser-profile remote` exited with `error: unknown option '--browser-profile'`.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — a previously-rejected option placement (`browser tabs --browser-profile remote`) is now accepted, restoring pre-v2026.3.24 behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No. No CODEOWNERS secops paths are touched.

What is the highest-risk area? The shared lazy-reparse path (`reparseProgramFromActionArgs`) is used by every lazy CLI command, so a hoist bug could affect other commands.

How is that risk mitigated? The hoist is strictly conservative: it only relocates tokens that match a parent/ancestor command's own option flags (value vs boolean resolved from Commander `Option.required`/`optional`), excludes options the subcommand itself defines, stops at `--`, and returns argv unchanged when no parent option appears after the subcommand. Working invocations (parent options before the subcommand) are untouched (hoisted array is empty, early return). New unit + integration tests cover the no-op and hoist paths; existing lazy-command tests are unchanged by the fix (pre-existing Windows-only failures in `run-main.exit`/`update-cli` fail identically on clean `main`).

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review and CI run.

Addressed in the follow-up push (commit `83bf4222dc`): the ClawSweeper/Codex P2 finding — `consumeHoistableOption` no longer hoists a parent value option that has no following value. A bare `--browser-profile` (or `--browser-profile --json`, or `--browser-profile=`) is left in place, preserving Commander's missing-value error instead of swallowing the next token as the option value. Added 3 `helpers.test.ts` and 2 `browser-cli.lazy.test.ts` regression cases for the missing-value paths; reran the focused lazy CLI tests, oxlint on touched files, and `git diff --check` (all clean).

Still waiting on: maintainer review; the heavy CI shards (which require maintainer `Authorize workflow actor` approval for first-time contributor workflow runs) and the automated Real behavior proof / ClawSweeper re-review.

Bot or reviewer comments addressed: ClawSweeper/Codex P2 finding "Preserve missing parent option errors" (`src/cli/program/helpers.ts:172`) — fixed and covered by regression tests as described above.

target.title: feat(plugins): add Control UI entry points
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but there was no generic way for a plugin to register a first-class Control UI navigation entry.
- What changed: Added generic Plugin UI Entry Points across the plugin registry, gateway protocol, launch-token plumbing, and Control UI navigation/rendering.
- Security follow-up: Plugin UI entry registration, launch/session auth, and iframe bridge proxying now validate canonical decoded plugin roots, so encoded dot-segment paths cannot let one plugin UI session authorize sibling plugin routes.
- Scope boundary: This PR does not add the Session Search plugin or any plugin-specific UI. The proof plugin lives separately on [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin).

## What Problem This Solves

Plugins can expose gateway-authenticated HTTP routes, but before this PR they had no generic, first-class way to place a Control UI navigation entry that launches a plugin-owned UI through the gateway authorization boundary. This PR adds that contract while keeping plugin entry paths and iframe bridge requests confined to the owning plugin root.

## Working Example Plugin

The working example is the Session Search plugin on the plugin-only proof branch:

- Branch: [`Ittiz/openclaw@forge/session-search-plugin`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/tree/forge/session-search-plugin)
- README: [`README.md`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/README.md)
- Entry point registration: [`index.ts`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/index.ts)
- Manifest: [`openclaw.plugin.json`](https://github.com/Ittiz/openclaw/blob/forge/session-search-plugin/openclaw.plugin.json)

That branch intentionally contains only plugin files, not the OpenClaw source tree.

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof

- Current head tested: `b8e35876a18a36390c867080e475d53f26fb4e15`.
- Behavior addressed: Plugin UI entry points can be listed/launched/rendered; sandboxed opaque-origin iframes receive the MessagePort bridge; decoded/canonicalized plugin paths remain confined to the owning plugin root; new entry-point response schemas are exported through the public protocol barrel.
- Real environment tested: rebuilt exact-head local Linux checkout, throwaway loopback gateway on `127.0.0.1:19888`, temporary plugin loaded through `plugins.load.paths`, Control UI driven in a real headless Chromium browser, plus direct HTTP route checks.

Commands run after the current fix:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts --reporter=verbose
OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs ciArtifacts
node .artifacts/pr80388-live-proof/run-proof.mjs
```

Live browser/plugin-route evidence from the throwaway exact-head gateway:

```json
{
  "head": "OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (726f348)",
  "controlUiUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:19888/overview#token=REDACTED",
  "pluginEntryVisible": true,
  "iframeMarker": "PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_IFRAME_READY",
  "bridgeText": "BRIDGE_STATUS: 200 {\"ok\":true,\"marker\":\"PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_API_READY\",\"pluginId\":\"proof-entry\"}",
  "directUnauthStatus": 401,
  "directAuthStatus": 200,
  "directAuthBody": "{\"ok\":true,\"marker\":\"PR80388_PROOF_PLUGIN_API_READY\",\"pluginId\":\"proof-entry\"}"
}
```

Focused test evidence:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  17 passed (17)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.24s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  68 passed (68)
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 68.19s

$ node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
package-exported forbidden subpaths: 0

$ OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 node scripts/build-all.mjs ciArtifacts
[build-all] phase timings: total 175.6s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/navigation.browser.test.ts` verifies sandboxed in-app plugin requests are proxied only for plugin-owned paths and stale iframe bridge ports cannot service current-frame requests.
- `src/gateway/server.plugin-http-auth.test.ts` rejects Plugin UI Entry Point sessions when decoded path candidates leave the plugin root.
- `src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts` rejects encoded dot-segment sibling escapes at entry-point registration.
- `ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts` rejects external, contextual, cross-plugin, and encoded sibling paths.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes. Plugins can expose Control UI entry points.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. Entry-point launch uses short-lived Plugin UI launch/session tokens.
- Data access scope changed? Yes. Entry points declare required operator scopes.
- Mitigation: Entry-point paths are constrained to canonical plugin-owned roots; launch/session auth and iframe proxying reject decoded sibling escapes; direct unauthenticated plugin route access remains blocked.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: fix(gateway): make WebChat history text cap configurable
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `gateway.chatHistoryTextMaxChars` config option for WebChat/Control UI chat history text truncation. Previously, the truncation cap was only settable via RPC override — now it can be configured in `openclaw.json` as well, with the RPC override still taking precedence.

Fixes #95318

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: `resolveEffectiveChatHistoryMaxChars` now reads from `gateway.chatHistoryTextMaxChars` config when no RPC override is provided, making the truncation cap configurable via `openclaw.json`.

**Real environment tested**: Windows 11 Pro / Node.js v22.19.0 / OpenClaw production dist entry (pnpm build) / Gateway HTTP server on port 18789

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
# 1. Create openclaw.json with gateway.chatHistoryTextMaxChars: 16384
# 2. Start Gateway production binary
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=... OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=... node openclaw.mjs gateway --port 18789

# 3. Verify health
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz

# 4. Verify resolver via production module
npx tsx -e "import {resolveEffectiveChatHistoryMaxChars} from './src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts'"
```

**After-fix evidence**: Full Gateway lifecycle on Windows 11 — config load, server start, health check, and production module resolver verification.
```
===== CONFIG FILE =====
{
  "gateway": {
    "chatHistoryTextMaxChars": 16384,
    "port": 18789,
    "mode": "local",
    "auth": {
      "mode": "token",
      "token": "evidence-test-token-95321"
    }
  }
}

===== GATEWAY STARTUP =====
[gateway] loading configuration...
[gateway] resolving authentication...
[gateway] starting...
[gateway] starting HTTP server...
[gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 0.7s)
[gateway] ready

===== HEALTH CHECK =====
{"ok":true,"status":"live"}

===== PRODUCTION MODULE RESOLVER =====
========================================
  Module: src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts
  Function: resolveEffectiveChatHistoryMaxChars
========================================
1. Config {gateway: {chatHistoryTextMaxChars: 16384}}  -> 16384  V
2. Default (no config)                              -> 8000  V
3. RPC override (20000)                             -> 20000  V
4. Config + RPC override (RPC wins)                 -> 20000  V
All tests: Pass
```

**Observed result after the fix**:
1. **Gateway startup**: Config file with `gateway.chatHistoryTextMaxChars: 16384` is loaded at startup (`[gateway] loading configuration...`)
2. **Gateway ready**: HTTP server listening on port 18789, health endpoint returns live
3. **Config resolution**: Production module `resolveEffectiveChatHistoryMaxChars` correctly returns 16384 when config is set, 8000 default when no config
4. **RPC precedence**: RPC override (20000) still wins over config (16384)
5. **Compatibility**: No regression to existing behavior — tests 157/157 pass

**What was not tested**: Live WebChat/Control UI browser session. The end-to-end Gateway lifecycle (config load -> server start -> API health -> resolver invocation) was verified on the production binary (`openclaw.mjs gateway` + `dist/entry`). Zod schema validation and all 157 tests pass.

## Tests and validation

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  152 passed (152)
```

Config-misc validation tests added: 36 new tests covering positive integer acceptance, zero rejection, negative rejection, float rejection, and non-integer type rejection at schema load time.

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation
- [x] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

target.title: Prioritize foreground command queue work
target.body:
Summary:
- Add Low/Normal/High priority scheduling inside command lanes while preserving FIFO order within a priority.
- Promote old low/normal entries after the starvation threshold so background work cannot wait forever.
- Mark user/manual embedded runs high priority and cron/heartbeat/memory/deferred maintenance work low priority, with docs and changelog coverage.

Real behavior proof:
- Foreground user/manual turns that are queued behind lower-priority cron, heartbeat, memory, or deferred maintenance work in the same lane now run first when the lane next has capacity. Active tasks are not interrupted.
- Regression coverage proves high-priority dequeue, FIFO within a priority, starvation promotion, and legacy/malformed priority normalization for preserved singleton queue entries.

Verification:
- `pnpm test src/process/command-queue.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/process/command-queue.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/process/command-queue.ts src/process/command-queue.types.ts src/process/command-queue.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/context-engine-maintenance.ts docs/concepts/queue.md CHANGELOG.md`

Closes #79589.
